1: # session-audit-002 — swarm inefficiency audit (pass 2)
2: 
3: Series: `session-audit-NNN` (Doc: docs/tasks/session-audit-series.md). Mechanism for ROI
4: **Priority 2** — mine sessions to simplify the protocol and cut wasted agent work.
5: Diagnose-and-enqueue ONLY; the fixes are the two tasks appended to PLAN.md, not implemented here.
6: 
7: ## Headline: the mineable corpus has collapsed — the engine window is EMPTY, but not because the swarm rested
8: 
9: `beehive audit --submodule beehive` (read-only, built from this worktree under the mandated static
10: env) returns an **EMPTY N-2 window**: zero un-audited sessions to mine. Per the series rule an empty
11: window means "no-op DONE, do not enqueue a successor — rest, no busy-spin." **That rule does not
12: apply here.** The window is empty because of a defect, not because the swarm has caught up: 729 real,
13: long-completed sessions exist since pass 1, but 96% of them never reached the audit-visible corpus.
14: Completing this pass as a no-op DONE would be a false "caught up" that silently buries an active
15: data-loss bug — so this pass diagnoses the collapse and enqueues its fix, exactly the Priority-2 job.
16: 
17: ## Corpus-integrity census (on-disk truth, `submodules/beehive/sessions/`)
18: 
19: | metric | value |
20: |--------|------:|
21: | session files on disk | 760 |
22: | **finalized** (real transcript, `submodule:·kind:·branch:` header — mineable) | **31** |
23: | **stubs** (185–191 B `beehive-session-branch:` placeholder — transcript never promoted to main) | **729 (96%)** |
24: | stubs whose stream branch still exists (transcript recoverable) | 632 |
25: | **stubs whose stream branch is already gone (transcript permanently LOST)** | **97** |
26: | finalized-session epoch range | 1782766865 → 1782841513 |
27: | stub epoch range | 1782804156 → 1783117266 |
28: | live session stream branches in the hive repo | 637 |
29: | finalize commits (`session: <sid>`) reachable in history | 1958 |
30: 
31: The engine parses only the 31 finalized transcripts; it emits **729 per-file
32: `no submodule/kind/branch header line` errors to stderr** (one per stub) and drops them. `Window()`
33: then applies N-2 and drops the pass-1 ledger, leaving nothing. The audit is structurally blind to 96%
34: of its own input.
35: 
36: ## F1 — Session transcripts are not being finalized to main; the run history is being destroyed → `session-transcript-finalize` (weight 64)
37: 
38: This is the dominant finding and it gates the entire self-audit workstream (and the ROI's "learn from
39: run history" goal) — no later pass can mine what never lands.
40: 
41: **Mechanism.** A running session streams its transcript as rapid commits to an isolated stream branch
42: (`internal/repo/session.go`); `main` carries only a **stub** at `sessions/<sid>.md` naming that
43: branch. At session end `Runner.finish` → `finalizeSession` (internal/swarm/swarm.go:769) is supposed
44: to squash the stream branch and **merge the durable final transcript onto main**, replacing the stub.
45: But that promotion is deliberately **best-effort**: on any failure it only prints a stderr WARNING and
46: leaves the stub in place with `SessionPublished=false` (swarm.go:762–773), because the transcript
47: "already lives on the stream branch." The audit engine reads **main**, not the stream branches — so
48: every session whose finalize-to-main step fails or never runs is invisible forever.
49: 
50: **Evidence it is failing, not resting.**
51: - **Sharp regression boundary.** Every finalized transcript is epoch ≤ **1782841513**; every session
52:   after that is a stub, straight through to **1783117266** (essentially now). Finalization worked
53:   through pass-1's audited window (which ended 1782803224) and then **regressed immediately after** —
54:   the first pass to run against the post-regression corpus (this one) is the first to see it.
55: - **Never promoted, not merely stale.** A 5-way sample across the 729 stubs (epoch-spread) shows
56:   **zero `session: <sid>` finalize commits in the full `--all` history** for any of them — these
57:   transcripts were never squashed to main, not promoted-then-reverted.
58: - **Active, irreversible loss.** Stream branches are reclaimed over time; **97 of the 729 stubs
59:   already have no surviving branch** — those transcripts are gone (not on main, not on any branch).
60:   The remaining 632 are one reclaim away from the same fate. Loss accelerates the longer this stands.
61: 
62: **Why it is the #1 agent-time saver (Priority-2 lens).** It is not a per-session turn cost; it is a
63: *pipeline-integrity* cost that zeroes the entire mining mechanism. Until it lands, `session-audit-003`
64: and every successor inherit an empty/defective window and can produce no findings — the whole
65: "mine sessions to cut wasted work" ROI lever is inert, and the raw material is being deleted while we
66: wait. Fixing it is the precondition for every future turn this series would ever save.
67: 
68: ## F2 — The audit silently treats a broken corpus as an empty one → `audit-stub-visibility` (weight 32)
69: 
70: Independent of F1's cause, the engine's response to 729 unfinalized sessions is to spew 729 stderr
71: parse errors and return an **empty window with no signal** — the same output it would give a genuinely
72: rested swarm. The two states are indistinguishable to an operator or to the next pass, which is how a
73: 96%-corpus-loss bug ran undetected from just after pass 1 until now. The stub is a *known, structured*
74: shape: `internal/repo.ParseSessionStub` already recognises it. The audit should **count** stubs, not
75: choke on them, and report a mineable-fraction with a loud warning when it is low — turning a silent
76: empty window into an actionable alarm so the series can never again be quietly starved.
77: 
78: **Why weight 32.** Cheap (reuses the existing stub detector), and it is the guard that makes F1-class
79: regressions self-announcing. Ranked below F1 because it improves *observability* of the defect rather
80: than restoring the corpus itself.
81: 
82: ## Ledger / trend — intentionally HELD at pass 1 (no pass-2 row written)
83: 
84: Pass 1 wrote `docs/audit/metrics.tsv` (29 rows) + `docs/audit/trend.tsv` (pass 1 baseline
85: `1  8  1044  3909320  12`, 130.5 turns/task). **This pass does NOT run `beehive audit --write` and
86: does NOT append a pass-2 row**, deliberately:
87: - The engine window is empty, so there are **zero sessions to mark audited** — the ledger cannot
88:   legitimately advance (the "covered sessions marked audited" bar is satisfied vacuously: none).
89: - A `--write` now would compute a pass-2 trend over only the **31 finalized** sessions (a read-only
90:   run yields `2  10  1119  4320151  12` → 111.9 turns/task), implying an *improvement* that is an
91:   artifact of the 96% the engine cannot see. Persisting that would be a fabricated metric — a
92:   swallowed-error violation. The honest ledger state is "pass 2 could not measure; corpus defective."
93: 
94: The trend resumes at the next pass that runs against a finalized corpus (`session-audit-003`, gated on
95: `session-transcript-finalize`).
96: 
97: ## Considered, NOT enqueued
98: 
99: - **Rerun/churn fix from the dispatched-session explosion.** Counting *dispatched* sessions per task
100:   (stub + finalized) shows extreme fan-out per delivered task — e.g. editor-safety-guards **71**,
101:   hive-hygiene-panel **64**, dashboard-cards **58**, editor-session-persist **48** — dozens of passes
102:   per single DONE task. This is real and severe, but (a) its accurate measurement is **corrupted by
103:   F1** (the per-task turn/retry truth lives in the unfinalized transcripts), and (b) it is the same
104:   reject→rework churn class that pass-1's **`handoff-verify-gate` already targets and is still TODO**.
105:   Enqueuing a churn fix now would duplicate an open task on corrupted data. Deferred to a post-finalize
106:   re-audit; `handoff-verify-gate` remains the standing lever.
107: - **Completion-miss / lost-race / abandoned fixes.** The engine flagged these over the 31 parseable
108:   sessions, but any window-level heuristic is meaningless while 96% of the window is missing. No such
109:   fix is fabricated this pass.
110: - **A tokens-per-honeybee (Priority-1, weight 128) pull-forward.** Neither enqueued fix is an
111:   injection/preamble/retrieval/history/model-routing token lever — F1 is corpus-pipeline integrity and
112:   F2 is audit observability. Both stay Priority-2 (64 / 32) per the ROI's tiering; no P1 promotion.
113: 
114: ## Enqueued fix tasks (appended to PLAN.md, this order = cost-saved order)
115: 1. `session-transcript-finalize` (weight 64) — Doc: docs/tasks/session-transcript-finalize.md
116: 2. `audit-stub-visibility` (weight 32) — Doc: docs/tasks/audit-stub-visibility.md
117: 
118: ## Successor
119: `session-audit-003` [TODO] appended (the batch — the real, non-empty backlog of 729 completed-but-
120: unmineable sessions — is non-empty; the empty *engine* window is the F1 defect, not a rested swarm).
121: It carries `deps=session-transcript-finalize` so the next pass does not burn another empty-window run
122: before the corpus is minable — the series' anti-busy-spin intent applied to the real blocker.

(End of file - total 122 lines)

# AGENTS.md — beehive submodule overlay

Submodule-local rules for honeybees working `submodules/beehive/` (the beehive
product source under `repo/`). Read alongside the repo-root `AGENTS.md` and
`HONEYBEE.md`.

## Sharing modes are an invariant — do not break them

Beehive components converge through git in one of two modes, **detected at
runtime from the repo's remotes with no configuration**: *local sharing* (shared
checkout, keep `main` a clean projection) and *remote sharing* (private checkout,
push/pull; push/pull failures are fatal). A swarm may be hybrid.

Before touching startup, publish, remote, or checkout-health code
(`cmd/honeybee/main.go`, `internal/git/git.go`, `internal/swarm`), read the design
contract:

- **`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`** — the two modes, the invariants, and the
  startup preflight guard (reset-dirty-with-WARNING; abort-before-tokens if the
  checkout can't be cleaned or the pull target is unreachable).

Do not add a mode flag or config; mode is derived from git remotes. Never author
in the live shared checkout — use a worktree and converge to `main`. A honeybee
`preflight` WARNING/ERROR in the logs is a real defect signal, not noise.

</system-reminder>

54: ## Findings (ranked by estimated agent-time saved)

56: ### F1 — Every session re-derives the host build/test environment → `runner-build-env` (weight 64)
57: The single largest sink, and it recurs in *every* session regardless of task. Across the

59: -latomic_asneeded` at the `go test -race` / cgo link step) and **21 hit `/tmp` tmpfs quota
60: exhaustion** ("disk quota exceeded"), forcing a manual `GOTMPDIR`/`TMPDIR` redirect; **28 hit at

62: 37 turns) alone carries **36 build-env references**. Every agent independently re-derives the
63: identical fix — build static (`CGO_ENABLED=0`) and redirect tmp+cache to a root-fs dir — which
64: **LOCALS.md already documents verbatim** but which is **not injected into the honeybee prompt**.

68: toolchain — not prompt echo.)

71:   rediscovery, plus repeated failed `go test -race` invocations.
72: - **Priority-2 class**: told-once + responsibility-removal. The runner already resolves
73:   per-submodule config (`config-layered`, DONE); have it EXPORT the mandated build/test env into
74:   the agent's process and state it once in the prompt, so no agent ever rediscovers it again.
75: 
76: ### F2 — Redundant reconcile loops re-folding an already-applied ROI delta → `reconcile-dedup-guard` (weight 32)
77: **8 of 29** window sessions are reconcile passes (**200 turns / 844,173 bytes**) and none deliver
78: a task. The engine flagged **5 as `reconcile_loop`** (`bee-reconcile-1782799963`, `-1782800042`,

82: (epochs …9963/…0042/…0084) and two more (…1302/…1722) after PLAN.md is already at `12f1f658`.

84: - **Inputs**: the 5 redundant passes ≈ **132 turns / ~581 KB** producing zero net plan progress.
85: - **Priority-2 class**: responsibility-removal. Before dispatching a reconcile, the runner should
86:   re-pull `main` and short-circuit when PLAN.md's `Beehive-ROI` stamp already prefixes the current
87:   ROI head — a deterministic early no-op. The prefix compare already exists (`reconcile-prefix-match`,
88:   DONE); this wires it into a pre-dispatch guard so no agent pass is spawned for a no-op.

91: The two most expensive task lineages are reruns, the ROI's named "near-total waste":
92: - **session-metrics-extract**: **5 sessions / 245 turns** (work 88 → review 49 → arbitrate 6 →
93:   arbitrate 47 → rework 55). The arbitration PAIR plus a second work pass is a reject→arbitrate→
94:   rework cycle — green on fixtures, then broke on the live corpus.

100:   (~100+ turns per cycle); the two lineages above account for **463 turns**.
101: - **Priority-2 class**: responsibility-removal. A deterministic pre-handoff verify gate — the
102:   runner runs `gofmt`/`go vet`/the task's own tests against the LIVE inputs (not only fixtures)
103:   before a task may flip to NEEDS-REVIEW — catches the mechanical-regression class that caused the
104:   churn, so a reviewer never spends a whole session rejecting a fmt/vet/test failure.

106: ### F4 — PLAN.md context bloat re-read every session → `plan-lean-task-card` (weight 16)
107: PLAN.md is **1,446 lines / 132,898 bytes**, and every honeybee re-reads it (it carries the claim

111: narrative is audit history, not task input.

113: - **Inputs**: ~130 KB of context loaded per session × 29 window sessions; the marginal re-read of

116:   the DONE `Impl`/`Review` prose to a sibling store (or fold it into the change doc that already

1: # session-audit-series  (seed task: session-audit-001)
2: 
3: ROI (Session self-audit → "The series" + "Output of each pass"): a recurring audit task evaluates the
4: N-2 window of completed sessions, "picks the next un-audited sessions ... never re-auditing one already
5: covered", and each pass emits an audit doc with per-session metrics + an inefficiency ranking by cost
6: saved, plus "A series of concrete fix tasks appended to PLAN.md ... The audit task only diagnoses and
7: enqueues; it does not implement the fixes itself."
8: 
9: This is the shared design doc for the whole `session-audit-NNN` series. Every pass task (`session-audit-001`,
10: `session-audit-002`, …) points here. Depends on **session-metrics-extract** for all numbers.
11: 
12: ## Why
13: The swarm should learn from its own run history and feed concrete fixes back into PLAN — continuously,
14: not once. Diagnosis is the deliverable; implementation is deferred to the enqueued fix tasks so a long
15: analytical pass never blocks on a long code change.
16: 
17: ## Priority-2 lens (ROI: "Mine sessions to simplify the protocol and cut wasted agent work")
18: This series is the mechanism for ROI Priority 2. The bar: anything an agent can be **told once, hinted
19: inline, summarized, or relieved of entirely** should be — every pass should SHRINK each agent's
20: responsibility surface. Scan each transcript through three questions and rank fixes by AGENT-TIME SAVED:
21: - Protocol confusion: agents re-deriving worktree/claim/publish rules, asking where a doc goes,
22:   mishandling submodule-vs-beehive-layer paths, or fighting git plumbing the runner is supposed to own.
23:   Each recurring confusion -> a crisper AGENTS.md rule, an inline prompt hint at the decision point, OR
24:   moving the work out of the agent into the runner.
25: - Time on unrelated tasks: turns spent on setup, exploration, or git mechanics unrelated to the actual
26:   change. Summarize/precompute it and hand the agent the result.
27: - Responsibility removal: if the runner can do something deterministically (path choices, stamping, branch
28:   naming, cleanup), take it OFF the agent's plate.
29: The enqueued fix tasks therefore skew toward prompt/AGENTS.md/runner changes (not only product code), each
30: still shipping its own design doc.
31: 
32: ## Recurrence model (no "recurring" status exists)
33: The series self-perpetuates by enqueuing its successor:
34: 1. When selected, ask `internal/audit` for the next un-audited batch in the N-2 window (two most recent
35:    sessions always excluded — they may be in-flight / under review / in arbitration and would mislead).
36: 2. If the batch is EMPTY: complete as a no-op DONE and do NOT enqueue a successor — the stream rests
37:    until new completed sessions land (a later reconcile or the next manual seed re-arms it). Do not
38:    busy-spin appending empty passes.
39: 3. If non-empty: produce the artifacts below, advance the ledger, and APPEND the successor
40:    `session-audit-<NNN+1>` [TODO] (attempts=0, deps=session-metrics-extract, Doc: this file).
41: Distinct per-pass ids are mandatory so each pass owns a unique audit doc and a unique completion/change
42: doc — never reuse an id.
43: 
44: ## Do (per pass NNN)
45: a. Run `internal/audit` over the N-2 window; get per-session metrics + per-task aggregates + the
46:    trend row for this pass. As of the six current sessions the first window (newest two excluded)
47:    covers bee-bootstrap-1782766865, links-graph-enforcement-1782767318, reconcile-1782772649,
48:    links-graph-enforcement-1782772942; the window advances as new sessions land.
49: b. Write the audit findings doc at EXACTLY `submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/session-audit-<NNN>.md`:
50:    - a per-session metrics table (turns, bytes, kind, taskid, flags);
51:    - findings with session references: why the long sessions are long; reimplementation churn
52:      (links-graph-enforcement ran three times — the canonical waste); lost-race / abandoned sessions
53:      and redundant reconcile loops; completion misses (wrong change-doc path, missing source-branch
54:      push, status left mid-phase); token/context bloat (oversized preambles, re-reading files,
55:      runner-owned git plumbing);
56:    - an inefficiency ranking ordered by estimated cost saved (turns/bytes/retries avoided);
57:    - the tracked metric trend vs prior passes (turns/bytes/retries per delivered task).
58: c. APPEND the concrete fix tasks to PLAN.md, ordered by cost saved, each `## <id> [TODO]
59:    <!-- attempts=0 deps=… weight=… -->` with a body and its OWN design doc under
60:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/tasks/<id>.md`. Tag deps where a fix rides existing code. Diagnose-and-enqueue
61:    ONLY — never implement a fix in this task.
62: d. Advance the audited ledger (mark the covered sessions audited) via `internal/audit` so it stays
63:    deterministic; leave PLAN.md parseable (plan.Parse round-trips).
64: e. Append the successor pass per the recurrence model.
65: f. Write the required completion/change doc at
66:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/bee-session-audit-<NNN>-session-audit-<NNN>.md` (the runner's completion-check
67:    path) carrying the `Beehive: session-audit-<NNN> <doc-path>` stamp; flip session-audit-<NNN> to
68:    NEEDS-REVIEW.
69: 
70: ## Verification
71: Largely analytical, but enforce: ledger advanced (covered sessions now marked audited, none
72: re-audited); every appended fix task has a matching docs/tasks/<id>.md; PLAN.md still parses; the audit
73: doc and the required completion doc both exist at their exact paths. Any helper code this pass adds
74: rides session-metrics-extract's tests.
75: 
76: ## Caveats
77: - Two-most-recent exclusion is non-negotiable; never audit a session that may still be in flight.
78: - The fix tasks are the product; resist implementing them here (a single pass that both diagnoses and
79:   fixes is exactly the long-session waste this workstream exists to kill).
80: - Cost-saved ranking is an estimate — show the inputs (turns/bytes/reruns) so it is auditable, not a
81:   bare number.
82: - Each fix task must ship its own design doc (protocol); a fix enqueued without a doc is incomplete.

<content>
1: package main
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"errors"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strconv"
9: 	"strings"

11: 	"github.com/spencerharmon/beehive/internal/audit"
12: 	"github.com/spencerharmon/beehive/internal/plan"
13: 	"github.com/spencerharmon/beehive/internal/repo"
14: 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15: )
16: 
17: // auditCmd runs the deterministic session-metrics extraction and N-2 selection
18: // the session-audit-NNN series consumes. By default it is read-only and prints
19: // machine-readable TSV (the un-audited window, the per-task aggregate, and the
20: // delivered-only trend gauge). With --write it appends the window + trend to the
21: // append-only ledger under submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/, marking those sessions
22: // audited. It reads only the beehive layer (sessions/, PLAN.md, docs/audit/).
23: func auditCmd() *cobra.Command {
24: 	var submodule string
25: 	var write bool
26: 	c := &cobra.Command{
27: 		Use:   "audit",
28: 		Short: "extract reproducible session metrics and select the next un-audited N-2 batch",
29: 		Args:  cobra.NoArgs,
30: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
31: 			root, err := findRoot()
32: 			if err != nil {
33: 				return err
34: 			}
35: 			rp, err := repo.Open(root)
36: 			if err != nil {
37: 				return err
38: 			}
39: 			sm, err := resolveSubmodule(rp, submodule)
40: 			if err != nil {
41: 				return err
42: 			}
43: 			// Submodule paths are already absolute (root-joined by Submodules()).
44: 			sessDir := sm.SessionsDir()
45: 			if _, err := os.Stat(sessDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
46: 				fmt.Printf("# no sessions for %s\n", sm.Name)
47: 				return nil
48: 			}
49: 			sessions, census, perr := parseSessions(sessDir)
50: 			if perr != nil {
51: 				return perr
52: 			}
53: 			delivered, err := deliveredSet(sm)
54: 			if err != nil {
55: 				return err
56: 			}
57: 			auditDir := filepath.Join(sm.Path, "docs", "audit")
58: 			led, err := audit.LoadLedger(auditDir)
59: 			if err != nil {
60: 				return err
61: 			}
62: 			pass := led.NextPass()
63: 			window := audit.Window(sessions, led.Audited())
64: 			// Aggregate over the FULL corpus so reruns/retries are true totals; the
65: 			// trend is the delivered-only cost gauge at this pass.
66: 			aggs := audit.Aggregate(sessions, delivered)
67: 			trend := audit.ComputeTrend(aggs, pass)
68: 
69: 			printCensus(census)
70: 			printWindow(window)
71: 			printAggregate(aggs)
72: 			printTrend(trend)
73: 			// Loud, byte-stable corpus-integrity alarm: an empty/sparse window over
74: 			// a corpus of unfinalized stubs is a finalization defect, not a rest.
75: 			// CorpusWarning yields "" (nothing printed) for a healthy corpus.
76: 			if w := census.CorpusWarning(len(window) == 0); w != "" {
77: 				fmt.Fprint(os.Stderr, w)
78: 			}
79: 
80: 			if write {
81: 				led.AppendPass(window, trend)
82: 				if err := led.Save(auditDir); err != nil {
83: 					return err
84: 				}
85: 				fmt.Printf("# wrote pass %d: %d sessions, %d delivered tasks -> %s\n",
86: 					pass, len(window), trend.DeliveredTasks, auditDir)
87: 			}
88: 			return nil
89: 		},
90: 	}
91: 	c.Flags().StringVar(&submodule, "submodule", "", "submodule to audit (default: the only one)")
92: 	c.Flags().BoolVar(&write, "write", false, "append this pass to the docs/audit ledger")
93: 	return c
94: }
95: 
96: // parseSessions runs the corpus census over sessDir and returns the finalized
97: // (mineable) sessions plus the full census (finalized/stub/malformed counts).
98: // Genuinely malformed files are surfaced on stderr (never swallowed) but must not
99: // zero the pass. Unfinalized stubs are NOT errors — they are counted in the
100: // census and drive the corpus-broken warning downstream. A hard error is returned
101: // only when nothing at all is usable or recognisable (no sessions AND no stubs),
102: // or when the directory itself cannot be read.
103: func parseSessions(sessDir string) ([]audit.Session, audit.Census, error) {
104: 	census, err := audit.ParseDirCensus(sessDir)
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return nil, audit.Census{}, err
107: 	}
108: 	if len(census.Errors) > 0 {
109: 		joined := errors.Join(census.Errors...)
110: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "beehive: audit: skipped %d malformed session(s):\n%v\n", len(census.Errors), joined)
111: 		if census.Finalized() == 0 && census.StubCount() == 0 {
112: 			// Nothing usable and nothing even recognisable as a stub — a real
113: 			// failure, not a rest and not an unfinalized corpus.
114: 			return nil, audit.Census{}, joined
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 	return census.Sessions, census, nil
118: }
119: 
120: // resolveSubmodule picks the named submodule, or the sole submodule when name is
121: // empty, erroring if the choice is ambiguous or unknown.
122: func resolveSubmodule(rp *repo.Repo, name string) (repo.Submodule, error) {
123: 	subs, err := rp.Submodules()
124: 	if err != nil {
125: 		return repo.Submodule{}, err
126: 	}
127: 	if len(subs) == 0 {
128: 		return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("no submodules in repo")
129: 	}
130: 	if name == "" {
131: 		if len(subs) != 1 {
132: 			names := make([]string, len(subs))
133: 			for i, s := range subs {
134: 				names[i] = s.Name
135: 			}
136: 			return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("--submodule required (have %s)", strings.Join(names, ", "))
137: 		}
138: 		return subs[0], nil
139: 	}
140: 	for _, s := range subs {
141: 		if s.Name == name {
142: 			return s, nil
143: 		}
144: 	}
145: 	return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown submodule %q", name)
146: }
147: 
148: // deliveredSet reads PLAN.md and returns the DONE taskids. A missing PLAN.md
149: // (un-bootstrapped submodule) means nothing delivered yet.
150: func deliveredSet(sm repo.Submodule) (map[string]bool, error) {
151: 	b, err := os.ReadFile(sm.PlanPath())
152: 	if err != nil {
153: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
154: 			return map[string]bool{}, nil
155: 		}
156: 		return nil, err
157: 	}
158: 	p, err := plan.Parse(string(b))
159: 	if err != nil {
160: 		return nil, err
161: 	}
162: 	return audit.DeliveredFromPlan(p), nil
163: }
164: 
165: func printCensus(c audit.Census) {
166: 	// The corpus census makes an empty audit window self-explaining:
167: 	// empty-because-audited (a rest) versus empty-because-unfinalized (a defect).
168: 	fmt.Println("# corpus census (finalized=mineable, stub=unfinalized, malformed=broken)")
169: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"total", "finalized", "stub", "malformed", "mineable_fraction"}, "\t"))
170: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
171: 		strconv.Itoa(c.Total()), strconv.Itoa(c.Finalized()), strconv.Itoa(c.StubCount()),
172: 		strconv.Itoa(c.ErrorCount()), strconv.FormatFloat(c.MineableFraction(), 'f', 3, 64),
173: 	}, "\t"))
174: 	if c.StubCount() > 0 {
175: 		fmt.Println("# unfinalized stubs (sid, branch)")
176: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"sid", "branch"}, "\t"))
177: 		for _, s := range c.Stubs {
178: 			fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{s.SID, s.Branch}, "\t"))
179: 		}
180: 	}
181: }
182: 
183: func printWindow(w []audit.Session) {
184: 	fmt.Println("# window (N-2, un-audited)")
185: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
186: 		"session_id", "epoch", "kind", "taskid", "bytes", "turns",
187: 		"aborted", "lost_race", "completion_miss", "reconcile_loop",
188: 	}, "\t"))
189: 	for _, s := range w {
190: 		h := s.Heuristics
191: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
192: 			s.ID, strconv.FormatInt(s.Epoch, 10), s.Kind, s.TaskID,
193: 			strconv.FormatInt(s.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(s.Turns),
194: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.Aborted), strconv.FormatBool(h.LostRace),
195: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.CompletionMiss), strconv.FormatBool(h.ReconcileLoop),
196: 		}, "\t"))
197: 	}
198: }
199: 
200: func printAggregate(aggs []audit.TaskAgg) {
201: 	fmt.Println("# per-task aggregate (full corpus)")
202: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"taskid", "reruns", "retries", "turns", "bytes", "delivered"}, "\t"))
203: 	for _, a := range aggs {
204: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
205: 			a.TaskID, strconv.Itoa(a.Reruns), strconv.Itoa(a.Retries),
206: 			strconv.Itoa(a.Turns), strconv.FormatInt(a.Bytes, 10), strconv.FormatBool(a.Delivered),
207: 		}, "\t"))
208: 	}
209: }
210: 
211: func printTrend(t audit.Trend) {
212: 	fmt.Println("# trend (delivered-only cost gauge)")
213: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
214: 		"pass", "delivered_tasks", "turns", "bytes", "retries",
215: 		"turns_per_task", "bytes_per_task", "retries_per_task",
216: 	}, "\t"))
217: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
218: 		strconv.Itoa(t.Pass), strconv.Itoa(t.DeliveredTasks),
219: 		strconv.Itoa(t.Turns), strconv.FormatInt(t.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(t.Retries),
220: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.TurnsPerTask(), 'f', 1, 64),
221: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.BytesPerTask(), 'f', 1, 64),
222: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.RetriesPerTask(), 'f', 2, 64),
223: 	}, "\t"))
224: }

(End of file - total 224 lines)
</content>

15: type TaskAgg struct {
16: 	TaskID    string
17: 	Sessions  []string // session IDs, epoch-sorted
18: 	Reruns    int      // == len(Sessions)
19: 	Retries   int      // Reruns - 1
20: 	Turns     int      // summed across sessions
21: 	Bytes     int64    // summed across sessions
22: 	Delivered bool
23: }
24: 
25: // Aggregate groups sessions by TaskID and marks each task delivered per the
26: // delivered set (taskid -> reached DONE). Sessions are assumed epoch-sorted (as
27: // ParseDir returns them); the result is sorted by TaskID for determinism.
28: func Aggregate(sessions []Session, delivered map[string]bool) []TaskAgg {
29: 	idx := map[string]*TaskAgg{}
30: 	var order []string
31: 	for _, s := range sessions {
32: 		a := idx[s.TaskID]
33: 		if a == nil {
34: 			a = &TaskAgg{TaskID: s.TaskID, Delivered: delivered[s.TaskID]}
35: 			idx[s.TaskID] = a
36: 			order = append(order, s.TaskID)
37: 		}
38: 		a.Sessions = append(a.Sessions, s.ID)
39: 		a.Turns += s.Turns
40: 		a.Bytes += s.Bytes
41: 	}
42: 	out := make([]TaskAgg, 0, len(order))
43: 	for _, id := range order {
44: 		a := idx[id]
45: 		a.Reruns = len(a.Sessions)
46: 		a.Retries = a.Reruns - 1
47: 		out = append(out, *a)
48: 	}
49: 	sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].TaskID < out[j].TaskID })
50: 	return out
51: }
52: 
53: // Trend is the tracked metric for one audit pass: the cost carried by DELIVERED
54: // tasks only, so successive passes can show whether the swarm is getting cheaper
55: // per shipped task. Totals are stored (not averages) to keep the ledger free of
56: // rounding; per-task averages are derived on read.
57: type Trend struct {
58: 	Pass           int
59: 	DeliveredTasks int
60: 	Turns          int   // total turns across delivered tasks
61: 	Bytes          int64 // total bytes across delivered tasks
62: 	Retries        int   // total retries across delivered tasks
63: }
64: 
65: // ComputeTrend sums the delivered-only cost for a pass.
66: func ComputeTrend(aggs []TaskAgg, pass int) Trend {
67: 	t := Trend{Pass: pass}
68: 	for _, a := range aggs {
69: 		if !a.Delivered {
70: 			continue
71: 		}
72: 		t.DeliveredTasks++
73: 		t.Turns += a.Turns
74: 		t.Bytes += a.Bytes
75: 		t.Retries += a.Retries
76: 	}
77: 	return t
78: }
79: 
80: // TurnsPerTask, BytesPerTask, RetriesPerTask are the derived per-delivered-task
81: // averages (zero when no delivered task, never a divide-by-zero panic).
82: func (t Trend) TurnsPerTask() float64   { return ratio(t.Turns, t.DeliveredTasks) }
83: func (t Trend) BytesPerTask() float64   { return ratio(int(t.Bytes), t.DeliveredTasks) }
84: func (t Trend) RetriesPerTask() float64 { return ratio(t.Retries, t.DeliveredTasks) }
85: 
86: func ratio(n, d int) float64 {
87: 	if d == 0 {
88: 		return 0
89: 	}
90: 	return float64(n) / float64(d)
91: }
92: 
93: // DeliveredFromPlan collects the taskids that reached DONE on main, the only
94: // definition of "delivered". It reads the parsed PLAN.md (beehive layer); the
95: // caller passes plan.Parse(planBytes).
96: func DeliveredFromPlan(p *plan.Plan) map[string]bool {
97: 	out := map[string]bool{}
98: 	for _, t := range p.Tasks {
99: 		if t.Status == plan.StatusDone {
100: 			out[t.ID] = true
101: 		}
102: 	}
103: 	return out
104: }
105: 
106: // Stub is one UNFINALIZED session file: a repo.SessionStub placeholder still on
107: // main while its transcript streams to a live branch. It is a known, expected
108: // shape (repo.ParseSessionStub recognises it), NOT corrupt noise — so the audit
109: // engine records it as an unfinalized stub (its sid and the branch it points to)
110: // rather than mis-filing it as a malformed parse error. A stub carries no
111: // mineable metrics yet; it becomes a finalized transcript when its session ends
112: // (that finalization is session-transcript-finalize's job, not audit's).
113: type Stub struct {
114: 	SID    string // session id == the file stem (name without the ".md")
115: 	Branch string // the live branch the stub points to
116: }
117: 
118: // Census is the corpus-integrity summary of a sessions directory: how many files
119: // are finalized (mineable) transcripts, how many are still unfinalized stubs, and
120: // how many are genuinely malformed. It exists to tell an EMPTY audit window apart
121: // from a rested swarm — empty-because-audited (healthy) versus
122: // empty-because-unfinalized (a corpus-loss defect that is byte-for-byte identical
123: // to a rest without this census). Reporting only: nothing here finalizes a stub.
124: type Census struct {
125: 	// Sessions are the finalized, mineable transcripts, epoch-sorted (exactly the
126: 	// slice ParseDir returns).
127: 	Sessions []Session
128: 	// Stubs are the unfinalized stub files, deterministically ordered by SID.
129: 	Stubs []Stub
130: 	// Errors are the genuinely malformed files (missing/garbled header, unreadable
131: 	// bytes) — NEVER stubs. One error per bad file, none swallowed.
132: 	Errors []error
133: }
134: 
135: // Finalized is the count of mineable transcripts.
136: func (c Census) Finalized() int { return len(c.Sessions) }
137: 
138: // StubCount is the number of unfinalized stub files.
139: func (c Census) StubCount() int { return len(c.Stubs) }
140: 
141: // ErrorCount is the number of genuinely malformed files.
142: func (c Census) ErrorCount() int { return len(c.Errors) }
143: 
144: // Total is every classified ".md" file: finalized + stubs + malformed.
145: func (c Census) Total() int { return c.Finalized() + c.StubCount() + c.ErrorCount() }
146: 
147: // MineableFraction is the share of the corpus that is finalized and thus
148: // mineable (finalized / total). An empty directory is defined as fully mineable
149: // (1.0): nothing is broken, there is simply nothing there — so a genuinely empty
150: // sessions dir never trips the corpus-broken warning.
151: func (c Census) MineableFraction() float64 {
152: 	total := c.Total()
153: 	if total == 0 {
154: 		return 1
155: 	}
156: 	return float64(c.Finalized()) / float64(total)
157: }
158: 
159: // LowMineableFraction is the threshold below which a corpus that carries stubs is
160: // judged "mostly unfinalized" and the loud warning fires even before the audit
161: // window is fully empty. Chosen so a handful of live-streaming sessions against a
162: // large finalized corpus stays quiet, while a corpus that is majority stubs (the
163: // 96%-corpus-loss defect) is caught.
164: const LowMineableFraction = 0.5
165: 
166: // CorpusBroken reports the "unfinalized, not rested" defect: unfinalized stubs
167: // exist AND either the mineable audit window is empty (nothing to mine, yet the
168: // corpus is NOT actually rested) or the mineable fraction has fallen below
169: // LowMineableFraction (the corpus is mostly unfinalized). A fully-finalized
170: // corpus (zero stubs) is never broken, so a healthy rested swarm — its window
171: // empty only because everything was audited — stays byte-for-byte silent.
172: func (c Census) CorpusBroken(windowEmpty bool) bool {
173: 	if len(c.Stubs) == 0 {
174: 		return false
175: 	}
176: 	return windowEmpty || c.MineableFraction() < LowMineableFraction
177: }
178: 
179: // CorpusWarning returns the loud, human-facing "corpus broken, not a rest" banner
180: // when the census+window is the unfinalized-not-rested defect (per CorpusBroken),
181: // or "" when the corpus is healthy. It is byte-stable: a fully-finalized corpus
182: // yields exactly zero bytes, so a caller can print the result unconditionally and
183: // a healthy pass stays silent. Reporting only — it names the defect and points at
184: // finalization; it does not finalize anything.
185: func (c Census) CorpusWarning(windowEmpty bool) string {
186: 	if !c.CorpusBroken(windowEmpty) {
187: 		return ""
188: 	}
189: 	var b strings.Builder
190: 	b.WriteString("================ CORPUS BROKEN, NOT A REST ================\n")
191: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "audit: %d of %d session files are UNFINALIZED stubs (mineable fraction %.2f, threshold %.2f).\n",
192: 		c.StubCount(), c.Total(), c.MineableFraction(), LowMineableFraction)
193: 	if windowEmpty {
194: 		b.WriteString("The audit window is EMPTY because the corpus is unfinalized, NOT because the swarm rested.\n")
195: 	}
196: 	b.WriteString("Do NOT read this as 'nothing to audit'. Finalize the streaming sessions so their transcripts become mineable (session-transcript-finalize).\n")
197: 	b.WriteString("===========================================================\n")
198: 	return b.String()
199: }

(End of file - total 199 lines)
</content>

1: // Package audit is the deterministic metrics engine for the recurring session
2: // self-audit (the session-audit-NNN series). It mines the raw honeybee
3: // transcripts under submodules/<sm>/sessions/<branch>-<epoch>[-<pid>].md into
4: // reproducible per-session and per-task numbers, selects the next un-audited
5: // batch in an N-2 window, and persists an append-only ledger under
6: // submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/ that doubles as the audited-marker and the
7: // tracked-trend record.
8: //
9: // Everything here is pure Go (CGO-free) and reads ONLY the beehive coordination
10: // layer (sessions/, docs/audit/, PLAN.md). It never reads or writes a target's
11: // repo/ checkout. The audit PASS only diagnoses and enqueues fix tasks; it must
12: // not recompute these values by hand, or the "trend is improving" claim is
13: // meaningless.
14: //
15: // # Turn-count rule (PINNED)
16: //
17: // A "turn" is one assistant message, counted as the number of lines exactly
18: // equal to "## assistant" (the producer marker emitted by
19: // internal/swarm.renderTranscript, "\n## %s\n\n" with role "assistant").
20: // Matching the exact line — not a "## " prefix — is required: assistant output
21: // embeds its own level-2 markdown headers (e.g. "## Notes", "## Goal") which
22: // would otherwise be miscounted. Validated against the real corpus this rule
23: // reproduces the ROI-cited spread exactly: min 14 turns (bee-reconcile-1782772649)
24: // to max 102 (bee-links-graph-enforcement-1782767318), inside 60–330 KB.
25: //
26: // # Heuristics are conservative flags, not inferences
27: //
28: // The protocol prompt itself (echoed into every transcript's user turn) contains
29: // the strings "lost the race", "ErrLost", and "STOP", so a whole-file text scan
30: // for lost-claim language false-positives on essentially every session. The only
31: // deterministic, producer-emitted abort signal is the trailing "## ⚠️ warning"
32: // block that internal/swarm.recorder.appendWarning writes when the runner aborts
33: // a session (wall/turn cap, lost claim, task removed). Abort/lost-race detection
34: // therefore keys off that block ONLY. reconcile-loop is a corpus-level property
35: // (adjacent reconcile sessions in epoch order) set by ParseDir, not by a single
36: // file.
37: package audit
38: 
39: // Session kinds, mirroring internal/swarm selection kinds as written into the
40: // transcript header line "submodule: <sm> · kind: <kind> · branch: <branch>".
41: const (
42: 	KindBootstrap   = "bootstrap"
43: 	KindWork        = "work"
44: 	KindReview      = "review"
45: 	KindArbitration = "arbitration"
46: 	KindReconcile   = "reconcile"
47: )
48: 
49: // Session is the extracted, reproducible metric record for one transcript file.
50: // All fields are derived deterministically from the file name and bytes; no
51: // value is eyeballed.
52: type Session struct {
53: 	ID        string // file stem, e.g. "bee-git-remote-ops-1782789603-253372" (may carry a trailing -<pid> suffix)
54: 	Epoch     int64  // first numeric segment after the "<branch>-" prefix in the file name
55: 	Submodule string // header "submodule:" field
56: 	Kind      string // header "kind:" field
57: 	Branch    string // header "branch:" field (authoritative; the file name's prefix)
58: 	TaskID    string // Branch minus the "bee-" prefix (the -<pid> suffix is never folded in)
59: 	Bytes     int64  // file size in bytes
60: 	Turns     int    // count of "## assistant" lines (PINNED turn rule)
61: 	UserTurns int    // count of "## user" lines (informational)
62: 
63: 	Heuristics Heuristics
64: }
65: 
66: // Heuristics are cheap, conservative review flags. A false "abandoned" is cheap;
67: // a fake "delivered" is not — so these only ever flag, never assert delivery.
68: type Heuristics struct {
69: 	// Aborted is true iff the runner appended a "## ⚠️ warning" block, the only
70: 	// deterministic on-disk signal that the session ended abnormally.
71: 	Aborted bool
72: 	// AbortReason is the first non-empty line of the warning block, "" if none.
73: 	AbortReason string
74: 	// LostRace is true when Aborted and the warning text (scoped to the block,
75: 	// never the prompt-polluted body) names a lost claim / forced reselect.
76: 	LostRace bool
77: 	// CompletionMiss flags a work session the runner cut off before handoff
78: 	// (Kind == work and Aborted): it never reached NEEDS-REVIEW.
79: 	CompletionMiss bool
80: 	// ReconcileLoop marks a reconcile session that is adjacent (in epoch order)
81: 	// to another reconcile session with no other-kind work between them — the
82: 	// reconcile-fired-repeatedly-without-progress waste pattern. Set by ParseDir.
83: 	ReconcileLoop bool
84: }

- Add a **streaming read path** to `internal/swarm` that consumes opencode's event stream and emits assistant tokens live, where **stream-end == turn-idle** (consistent with the just-shipped `opencode-turn-poll`), falling back to the poll path when unsupported.

- Architecture reality: beehived does **not** hold a live opencode session. So a browser-facing SSE surface is a *different* transport than the swarm↔opencode streaming.

#!/bin/bash
# For each session with a per-turn-timeout warning, does the last assistant
# message before the warning contain an interactive question/ask tool call?
timeout_stall=0; ends_interactive=0; ends_other=0
for f in sessions/*.md; do
  # real warning block only (line starts with "## ⚠️ warning")
  grep -q "^## ⚠️ warning" "$f" || continue
  reason=$(awk '/^## ⚠️ warning/{f=1;next} f&&NF{print;exit}' "$f")
  case "$reason" in
    *"per-turn timeout"*) ;;
    *) continue ;;
  esac
  timeout_stall=$((timeout_stall+1))
  # text between the LAST "## assistant" and the warning
  tail_block=$(awk '
    /^## assistant/{start=NR}
    /^## ⚠️ warning/{w=NR; exit}
    {lines[NR]=$0}
    END{for(i=start;i<w;i++) print lines[i]}' "$f")
  if printf '%s' "$tail_block" | grep -qE "🔧 (question|ask)|\"questions\"[[:space:]]*:"; then
    ends_interactive=$((ends_interactive+1))
  else
    ends_other=$((ends_other+1))
  fi
done
echo "per-turn-timeout stalls: $timeout_stall"
echo "  last assistant msg calls interactive question/ask tool: $ends_interactive"
echo "  other: $ends_other"
EOF

> 
> The issue is a race condition in the review dispatch system: when a review pass approves a task and sets it to DONE, that change doesn't propagate back to main before the runner selects the same NEEDS-REVIEW task again, so multiple review passes end up working on the same task concurrently. Each one does the full review work independently, re-approving something that's already been approved, wasting enormous amounts of effort. For editor-safety-guards alone, 61 review passes were dispatched when only 1 was needed — that's roughly 60 wasted passes times 40-70 turns each, thousands of wasted turns on a single task. This redundant re-review pattern appears across multiple tasks like hive-hygiene-panel, dashboard-cards, and multi-repo-config-migration, making it likely the biggest source of wasted turns in the entire corpus.
> 
> The core question is whether the approval commits are actually failing to merge to main, which would keep the task stuck in NEEDS-REVIEW and trigger endless re-dispatch cycles.  Since these review passes are serial (hours apart, not concurrent), it's not a race condition—it's the same task being re-selected and re-reviewed because the approval never landed on main to mark it DONE. The fix is a review-dedup-guard similar to the reconcile dedup: before dispatching a review pass, verify the task is still NEEDS-REVIEW on fresh main and check for an active review claim with a valid heartbeat. The safety guards data shows zero interactive stalls among those 61 review passes, and the three sessions I examined all approved cleanly, so this isn't the same stall issue — it's genuinely redundant re-review where approvals aren't sticking to the task state. 1: package audit
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bufio"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strconv"
9: 	"strings"
10: )
11: 
12: // Ledger filenames under submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/. metrics.tsv is the
13: // per-session record (one row per audited session; its presence IS the
14: // audited-marker). trend.tsv is the per-pass tracked metric. Both are
15: // append-only and tab-separated for clean git diffs and exact round-trips.
16: const (
17: 	MetricsFile = "metrics.tsv"
18: 	TrendFile   = "trend.tsv"
19: )
20: 
21: var (
22: 	metricsHdr = []string{
23: 		"pass", "session_id", "epoch", "submodule", "kind", "branch", "taskid",
24: 		"bytes", "turns", "user_turns", "aborted", "lost_race", "completion_miss",
25: 		"reconcile_loop", "abort_reason",
26: 	}
27: 	trendHdr = []string{"pass", "delivered_tasks", "turns", "bytes", "retries"}
28: )
29: 
30: // MetricRow is one audited session pinned to the pass that recorded it.
31: type MetricRow struct {
32: 	Pass    int
33: 	Session Session
34: }
35: 
36: // Ledger is the parsed append-only audit record. It is the audited-state (which
37: // sessions have been seen) and the trend history (cost per delivered task per
38: // pass) in one place.
39: type Ledger struct {
40: 	Metrics []MetricRow
41: 	Trends  []Trend
42: }
43: 
44: // LoadLedger reads the ledger from dir. A missing dir or missing file is an empty
45: // (not erroneous) ledger: the first pass starts from nothing.
46: func LoadLedger(dir string) (*Ledger, error) {
47: 	l := &Ledger{}
48: 	if err := loadMetrics(filepath.Join(dir, MetricsFile), l); err != nil {
49: 		return nil, err
50: 	}
51: 	if err := loadTrend(filepath.Join(dir, TrendFile), l); err != nil {
52: 		return nil, err
53: 	}
54: 	return l, nil
55: }
56: 
57: // Audited returns the set of session IDs already in the ledger.
58: func (l *Ledger) Audited() map[string]bool {
59: 	out := make(map[string]bool, len(l.Metrics))
60: 	for _, m := range l.Metrics {
61: 		out[m.Session.ID] = true
62: 	}
63: 	return out
64: }
65: 
66: // NextPass is the pass number for the next AppendPass: one past the highest
67: // recorded pass, or 1 for an empty ledger.
68: func (l *Ledger) NextPass() int {
69: 	max := 0
70: 	for _, m := range l.Metrics {
71: 		if m.Pass > max {
72: 			max = m.Pass
73: 		}
74: 	}
75: 	for _, t := range l.Trends {
76: 		if t.Pass > max {
77: 			max = t.Pass
78: 		}
79: 	}
80: 	return max + 1
81: }
82: 
83: // AppendPass records sessions and the pass trend under trend.Pass. Sessions are
84: // appended in the given order (the caller passes the epoch-sorted window).
85: func (l *Ledger) AppendPass(sessions []Session, trend Trend) {
86: 	for _, s := range sessions {
87: 		l.Metrics = append(l.Metrics, MetricRow{Pass: trend.Pass, Session: s})
88: 	}
89: 	l.Trends = append(l.Trends, trend)
90: }
91: 
92: // Save writes both TSV files under dir (created if absent), in slice order.
93: func (l *Ledger) Save(dir string) error {
94: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
95: 		return err
96: 	}
97: 	if err := writeTSV(filepath.Join(dir, MetricsFile), metricsHdr, l.metricRows()); err != nil {
98: 		return err
99: 	}
100: 	return writeTSV(filepath.Join(dir, TrendFile), trendHdr, l.trendRows())
101: }
102: 
103: func (l *Ledger) metricRows() [][]string {
104: 	rows := make([][]string, 0, len(l.Metrics))
105: 	for _, m := range l.Metrics {
106: 		s := m.Session
107: 		h := s.Heuristics
108: 		rows = append(rows, []string{
109: 			strconv.Itoa(m.Pass), s.ID, strconv.FormatInt(s.Epoch, 10),
110: 			s.Submodule, s.Kind, s.Branch, s.TaskID,
111: 			strconv.FormatInt(s.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(s.Turns), strconv.Itoa(s.UserTurns),
112: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.Aborted), strconv.FormatBool(h.LostRace),
113: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.CompletionMiss), strconv.FormatBool(h.ReconcileLoop),
114: 			tsvSafe(h.AbortReason),
115: 		})
116: 	}
117: 	return rows
118: }
119: 
120: func (l *Ledger) trendRows() [][]string {
121: 	rows := make([][]string, 0, len(l.Trends))
122: 	for _, t := range l.Trends {
123: 		rows = append(rows, []string{
124: 			strconv.Itoa(t.Pass), strconv.Itoa(t.DeliveredTasks),
125: 			strconv.Itoa(t.Turns), strconv.FormatInt(t.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(t.Retries),
126: 		})
127: 	}
128: 	return rows
129: }
130: 
131: func loadMetrics(path string, l *Ledger) error {
132: 	recs, err := readTSV(path, metricsHdr)
133: 	if err != nil || recs == nil {
134: 		return err
135: 	}
136: 	for _, r := range recs {
137: 		pass, err := atoi(r[0], path)
138: 		if err != nil {
139: 			return err
140: 		}
141: 		epoch, err := atoi64(r[2], path)
142: 		if err != nil {
143: 			return err
144: 		}
145: 		bytes, err := atoi64(r[7], path)
146: 		if err != nil {
147: 			return err
148: 		}
149: 		turns, err := atoi(r[8], path)
150: 		if err != nil {
151: 			return err
152: 		}
153: 		userTurns, err := atoi(r[9], path)
154: 		if err != nil {
155: 			return err
156: 		}
157: 		aborted, lostRace, completionMiss, reconcileLoop, err := parseBools(r[10], r[11], r[12], r[13], path)
158: 		if err != nil {
159: 			return err
160: 		}
161: 		l.Metrics = append(l.Metrics, MetricRow{
162: 			Pass: pass,
163: 			Session: Session{
164: 				ID: r[1], Epoch: epoch, Submodule: r[3], Kind: r[4], Branch: r[5], TaskID: r[6],
165: 				Bytes: bytes, Turns: turns, UserTurns: userTurns,
166: 				Heuristics: Heuristics{
167: 					Aborted: aborted, LostRace: lostRace,
168: 					CompletionMiss: completionMiss, ReconcileLoop: reconcileLoop,
169: 					AbortReason: r[14],
170: 				},
171: 			},
172: 		})
173: 	}
174: 	return nil
175: }
176: 
177: func loadTrend(path string, l *Ledger) error {
178: 	recs, err := readTSV(path, trendHdr)
179: 	if err != nil || recs == nil {
180: 		return err
181: 	}
182: 	for _, r := range recs {
183: 		pass, err := atoi(r[0], path)
184: 		if err != nil {
185: 			return err
186: 		}
187: 		dt, err := atoi(r[1], path)
188: 		if err != nil {
189: 			return err
190: 		}
191: 		turns, err := atoi(r[2], path)
192: 		if err != nil {
193: 			return err
194: 		}
195: 		bytes, err := atoi64(r[3], path)
196: 		if err != nil {
197: 			return err
198: 		}
199: 		retries, err := atoi(r[4], path)
200: 		if err != nil {
201: 			return err
202: 		}
203: 		l.Trends = append(l.Trends, Trend{
204: 			Pass: pass, DeliveredTasks: dt, Turns: turns, Bytes: bytes, Retries: retries,
205: 		})
206: 	}
207: 	return nil
208: }
209: 
210: // readTSV returns the data rows (header validated, excluded). A missing file
211: // yields (nil, nil): an empty ledger, not an error.
212: func readTSV(path string, header []string) ([][]string, error) {
213: 	f, err := os.Open(path)
214: 	if err != nil {
215: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
216: 			return nil, nil
217: 		}
218: 		return nil, err
219: 	}
220: 	defer f.Close()
221: 	sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
222: 	sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 1<<20)
223: 	var rows [][]string
224: 	first := true
225: 	for sc.Scan() {
226: 		line := strings.TrimRight(sc.Text(), "\r")
227: 		if line == "" {
228: 			continue
229: 		}
230: 		fields := strings.Split(line, "\t")
231: 		if first {
232: 			if len(fields) != len(header) {
233: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: header has %d cols, want %d", path, len(fields), len(header))
234: 			}
235: 			first = false
236: 			continue
237: 		}
238: 		if len(fields) != len(header) {
239: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: row has %d cols, want %d", path, len(fields), len(header))
240: 		}
241: 		rows = append(rows, fields)
242: 	}
243: 	if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
244: 		return nil, err
245: 	}
246: 	return rows, nil
247: }
248: 
249: func writeTSV(path string, header []string, rows [][]string) error {
250: 	var b strings.Builder
251: 	b.WriteString(strings.Join(header, "\t"))
252: 	b.WriteByte('\n')
253: 	for _, r := range rows {
254: 		b.WriteString(strings.Join(r, "\t"))
255: 		b.WriteByte('\n')
256: 	}
257: 	return os.WriteFile(path, []byte(b.String()), 0o644)
258: }
259: 
260: // tsvSafe strips tab/newline so a free-text field cannot break the TSV grid.
261: func tsvSafe(s string) string {
262: 	return strings.NewReplacer("\t", " ", "\n", " ", "\r", " ").Replace(s)
263: }
264: 
265: func atoi(s, path string) (int, error) {
266: 	n, err := strconv.Atoi(s)
267: 	if err != nil {
268: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad int %q: %w", path, s, err)
269: 	}
270: 	return n, nil
271: }
272: 
273: func atoi64(s, path string) (int64, error) {
274: 	n, err := strconv.ParseInt(s, 10, 64)
275: 	if err != nil {
276: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad int %q: %w", path, s, err)
277: 	}
278: 	return n, nil
279: }
280: 
281: func parseBools(a, b, c, d, path string) (bool, bool, bool, bool, error) {
282: 	pa, err := strconv.ParseBool(a)
283: 	if err != nil {
284: 		return false, false, false, false, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad bool %q: %w", path, a, err)
285: 	}
286: 	pb, err := strconv.ParseBool(b)
287: 	if err != nil {
288: 		return false, false, false, false, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad bool %q: %w", path, b, err)
289: 	}
290: 	pc, err := strconv.ParseBool(c)
291: 	if err != nil {
292: 		return false, false, false, false, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad bool %q: %w", path, c, err)
293: 	}
294: 	pd, err := strconv.ParseBool(d)
295: 	if err != nil {
296: 		return false, false, false, false, fmt.Errorf("audit: %s: bad bool %q: %w", path, d, err)
297: 	}
298: 	return pa, pb, pc, pd, nil
299: }

(End of file - total 299 lines)
</content>
```

1: # reconcile-dedup-guard
2: 
3: ROI Priority 2 ("Responsibility removal: if the runner can do something deterministically … take it
4: off the agent's plate"; Session self-audit: "redundant reconcile loops (two reconcile runs back to

7: ## Why
8: Audit pass 1 (docs/audit/session-audit-001.md, F2): 8 of 29 window sessions are reconcile passes
9: (200 turns / 844,173 bytes) delivering nothing; the engine flagged 5 as `reconcile_loop`
10: (`bee-reconcile-1782799963`, `-1782800042`, `-1782800084`, `-1782801302`, `-1782801722`). All five
11: reference BOTH the prior ROI stamp `45ea5e90` and the new `12f1f658…` — they re-fold the SAME ROI
12: delta an earlier pass already stamped into PLAN.md; three fire within ~2 minutes, two more after
13: PLAN.md is already at `12f1f658`. ~132 turns / ~581 KB of zero-progress agent passes the runner
14: should never have spawned.
15: 
16: ## Why it happens
17: `reconciled()` (internal/swarm/swarm.go) reports reconcile-done from the LOCAL PLAN.md stamp vs the
18: LOCAL ROI head. When several dispatch cycles evaluate against a not-yet-pulled / just-changed tree,
19: the selector keeps re-emitting a reconcile even though the delta is already folded and stamped. There
20: is no pre-dispatch idempotency check against the freshest published state.
21: 
22: ## Do
23: Make "reconcile already applied" a deterministic pre-dispatch no-op — never an agent pass:
24: 1. Before selecting/dispatching a reconcile, re-pull `main` (`git.Pull --ff-only`, from git-remote-ops)
25:    so evaluation runs against the freshest tip.
26: 2. Reuse the existing prefix compare (reconcile-prefix-match, DONE): if PLAN.md's `Beehive-ROI` stamp
27:    already prefixes the current ROI head, short-circuit — do NOT spawn a reconcile session (log a
28:    skipped/no-op, do not flip anything).
29: 3. Guard against overlapping dispatch: once a reconcile has stamped the current ROI head, subsequent
30:    selection must not re-pick reconcile for the same head (single-flight / stamp-equality gate) until
31:    ROI.md advances again.
32: 
33: ## Tests
34: - select/swarm: with PLAN.md stamped at (a prefix of) the current ROI head, the pre-dispatch check
35:   returns "no reconcile needed" and no session is spawned; with ROI advanced past the stamp, exactly
36:   one reconcile is emitted.
37: - back-to-back: simulate two selection cycles at the same ROI head after a reconcile stamped it —
38:   assert the second does NOT emit a reconcile (the loop the audit found).
39: - ff-pull: the pre-check pulls the tracked tip before evaluating (fake-origin: a stamp landed on
40:   origin is seen and suppresses a local re-reconcile).
41: 
42: ## Caveats
43: - Orthogonal to `publish-advance-guard` (which verifies the publish reached origin): that closes the
44:   local-vs-origin completion gap; this stops re-dispatching a reconcile whose delta is already folded.
45:   If both land, keep the prefix compare and evaluate against the PULLED tip.
46: - Do not suppress a legitimately-needed reconcile when ROI.md genuinely advanced — key only off
47:   stamp-prefixes-head at the freshest tip, never off a timer.
48: - Rides reconcile-prefix-match + git-remote-ops (both DONE); no new dependency task.

60: // SetModel selects the provider/model for sessions opened after this call. The
61: // runner calls it at dispatch to route a pass to a per-kind model (cheap vs
62: // strong); the runner never passes an empty model, so this only ever narrows to
63: // a configured choice. A no-op relative to the field it sets.
64: func (o *Opencode) SetModel(model string) { o.Model = model }
65: 
66: // Open creates a server session for the working directory dir (an absolute path;
67: // opencode takes the cwd from the ?directory= query, not a body field) under the
68: // given system prompt, WITHOUT sending a first message. The caller drives turns
69: // via Session.Prompt, which lets a recorder start before the first (often long)
70: // turn.
71: func (o *Opencode) Open(ctx context.Context, dir, system string) (Session, error) {
72: 	body := map[string]any{
73: 		"agent": "build", // primary agent that can edit/run, not read-only chat
74: 		// auto-approve all tool actions so the honeybee runs autonomously
75: 		"permission": []map[string]any{{"permission": "*", "pattern": "**", "action": "allow"}},
76: 	}
77: 	var created struct {
78: 		ID string `json:"id"`
79: 	}
80: 	if err := o.post(ctx, "/session", dir, body, &created); err != nil {
81: 		return nil, err
82: 	}
83: 	if created.ID == "" {
84: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opencode: empty session id")
85: 	}
86: 	return &ocSession{oc: o, id: created.ID, dir: dir, system: system}, nil
87: }
88: 
89: // NewSession creates a session and sends the first prompt, returning its reply.
90: // Convenience for callers that don't record the first turn (e.g. the editor,
91: // which reads the changed file from disk rather than the opencode transcript).
92: func (o *Opencode) NewSession(ctx context.Context, dir, system, first string) (Session, string, error) {
93: 	s, err := o.Open(ctx, dir, system)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return nil, "", err
96: 	}
97: 	reply, err := s.Prompt(ctx, first)
98: 	if err != nil {
99: 		return nil, "", err
100: 	}
101: 	return s, reply, nil
102: }

188: // assistant's concatenated text parts.
189: //
190: // opencode's POST /session/{id}/message returns as soon as the turn is ACCEPTED
191: // (fire-and-forget), echoing the assistant message stub but NOT waiting for the
192: // model to act. Returning there would let the caller — and, in the runner, the
193: // deterministic completion check — race ahead of the agent, so every turn would
194: // "finish" in milliseconds. We therefore capture the assistant message id +
195: // completion marker from the accept reply; if it is already finished (a server
196: // that ran the turn synchronously) we use it directly, otherwise we poll the
197: // message list until that assistant message reports completed. ctx (the runner's
198: // per-turn timeout / WallCap) bounds the wait and poll errors are surfaced, never
199: // swallowed.
200: func (s *ocSession) Prompt(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
201: 	prov, model, _ := strings.Cut(s.oc.Model, "/")
202: 	body := map[string]any{
203: 		"agent":  "build",
204: 		"system": s.system,
205: 		"model":  map[string]any{"providerID": prov, "modelID": model},
206: 		"parts":  []map[string]any{{"type": "text", "text": text}},
207: 		// Disable opencode's interactive elicitation tool. A honeybee pass is
208: 		// headless: nothing is attached to answer a `question` (elicitation)
209: 		// prompt, so a model that calls it blocks the whole turn until the
210: 		// per-turn timeout kills the pass — discarding the turn's work and
211: 		// stranding the task claim until TTL GC. The only sanctioned way for a
212: 		// pass to reach a human is `beehive task human` (NEEDS-HUMAN), which
213: 		// ends the pass cleanly; the elicitation tool must never be offered.
214: 		// opencode's message API takes a per-message tools map ({name: enabled})
215: 		// that overrides the agent's tool set for this turn.
216: 		"tools": map[string]bool{"question": false},
217: 	}
218: 	// Model knobs from the resolved (layered) config. Only sent when explicitly
219: 	// configured (non-zero): an unset knob leaves the request byte-identical to
220: 	// the old default path, so the backend's own default applies.
221: 	if s.oc.Temperature != 0 {
222: 		body["temperature"] = s.oc.Temperature
223: 	}
224: 	if s.oc.MaxTokens != 0 {
225: 		body["maxTokens"] = s.oc.MaxTokens
226: 	}
227: 	var reply struct {
228: 		Info struct {
229: 			ID   string `json:"id"`
230: 			Time struct {
231: 				Completed float64 `json:"completed"`
232: 			} `json:"time"`
233: 		} `json:"info"`
234: 		Parts []struct {
235: 			Type string `json:"type"`
236: 			Text string `json:"text"`
237: 		} `json:"parts"`
238: 	}
239: 	// Drive the turn under an idle watchdog. opencode runs a turn SYNCHRONOUSLY:
240: 	// POST /message blocks for the entire turn (seen live: a trivial turn returns

done < /tmp/opencode/window-ids.txt
echo "interactive-stalls BEFORE fix($FIX)=$before  AFTER=$after"
echo "epoch range of interactive-stalls: $minep .. $maxep"
EOF

132: 1: # journal-activity-stream — always-on concise activity stream to the journal
133: 2: 
134: 3: Task: `journal-activity-stream` (submodule `beehive`) · branch
135: 4: `bee-journal-activity-stream` · pointer bumped to `053580d`.
136: 5: 
137: 6: ## Problem (observability/correctness blocker)
138: 7: 
139: 8: A scheduled pass is launched as `systemd-run … honeybee <repo>` with **no
140: 9: `--debug`**. Every live window into a pass was gated on that flag:
141: 10: 
142: 11: - `cmd/honeybee`: `runner.Debug`/`oc.Debug = os.Stderr` only `if debug`.
143: 12: - `internal/swarm/record.go`: the recorder teed activity (`streamDebug`) only when
144: 13:   `rc.debug != nil`.
145: 14: - `internal/swarm/swarm.go`: the `opening session… kind=…` line only when
146: 15:   `r.Debug != nil`; there was **no per-turn boundary marker** at all.
147: 16: 
148: 17: So a scheduled pass emitted only the runner's terminal warning lines and **nothing
149: 18: about what the agent was doing**. With a stub-only transcript, a stalled pass was
150: 19: *doubly invisible* — exactly how a 100%-stall task ran unseen for days
151: 20: (`journalctl --user -t honeybee` showed start/stop, no activity).
152: 21: 
153: 22: ## Change — split the recorder's always-on concise sink from the debug-only verbose sink
154: 23: 
155: 24: Two independent, nil-safe sinks (the concise content already existed in
156: 25: `streamDebug`; it was all routed to one debug-only writer):
157: 26: 
158: 27: - **activity (ALWAYS-ON, concise)** — user/step boundaries, tool-call **names +
159: 28:   concise input**, tool **errors**, recorder stream-health notices. Wired to
160: 29:   `os.Stderr` on every real pass.
161: 30: - **debug (`--debug` only, VERBOSE)** — assistant text + reasoning deltas and full
162: 31:   tool **output**/completion. A *superset*: under `--debug` both sinks are stderr,
163: 32:   so stderr carries concise + verbose (each line routed to exactly one sink — no
164: 33:   duplication).
165: 34: 
166: 35: Files:
167: 36: 
168: 37: - `internal/swarm/record.go` — `recorder` gains `activity io.Writer`; `streamDebug`
169: 38:   → `stream()` routes concise→activity, verbose→debug via nil-safe
170: 39:   `activityf`/`debugf`/`debugln`; `streamDelta` (text/reasoning) is debug-only;
171: 40:   `render` tees when *either* sink is live (`streaming()`); loop stream-fallback
172: 41:   notices move to the always-on sink. **No sink writes `rc.path`** ⇒ durable
173: 42:   session-branch transcript byte-identical.
174: 43: - `internal/swarm/swarm.go` — `Runner` gains `Activity io.Writer`; recorder built
175: 44:   with `activity: r.Activity`; the `opening session… kind=…` line and a new
176: 45:   **per-turn boundary** `[honeybee] turn N/max <kind>` go to the always-on sink via
177: 46:   `r.activityf`.
178: 47: - `cmd/honeybee/main.go` — `runner.Activity = os.Stderr` **unconditionally**;
179: 48:   `runner.Debug`/`oc.Debug` stay behind `--debug` (`oc.Debug` = HTTP-level verbose,
180: 49:   correctly debug-only). `res.Warning` (warning/GC reason) was, and stays, printed
181: 50:   unconditionally by `main`.
182: 51: 
183: 52: `finalizeSession` / `reclaimSourceBranch` untouched.
184: 53: 
185: 54: ## Result
186: 55: 
187: 56: A scheduled pass (no `--debug`) now streams to `journalctl --user -t honeybee`:
188: 57: pass kind at open, `[honeybee] turn N/max <kind>` per turn, `· <tool> <input>` per
189: 58: tool call, `✗ <tool>: <err>` on tool errors, plus the terminal warning/GC reason —
190: 59: so a stalled pass reveals which turn/tool it hung on, live. `--debug` still adds
191: 60: the full-transcript tee on top.
192: 61: 
193: 62: ## Tests (`internal/swarm/record_test.go`)
194: 63: 
195: 64: - `TestRecorderConciseActivityAlwaysOn` — activity set, **debug nil**: concise
196: 65:   markers fire; verbose sentinels (reasoning/text/tool-output) do **not** leak in.
197: 66:   The no-Debug proof; cannot pass before the split.
198: 67: - `TestRecorderDebugAddsVerboseSuperset` — both sinks: concise unchanged AND the
199: 68:   verbose sink adds reasoning/text/output on top.
200: 69: - `TestRecorderTranscriptByteIdenticalRegardlessOfSinks` — durable file identical
201: 70:   with vs without the concise sink.
202: 71: 
203: 72: `go build ./...` + `go test ./...` green under `CGO_ENABLED=0`; `gofmt`/`go vet`
204: 73: clean.
205: 74: 
206: 75: ## Branch/publish note
207: 76: 
208: 77: `origin/bee-journal-activity-stream` holds a divergent orphan (`fb3e3cfa`) from a
209: 78: prior GC'd attempt, so a plain push of `053580d` is rejected non-fast-forward. Per
210: 79: HONEYBEE.md (no force-push, no origin reconcile) the commit is left on the local
211: 80: branch for the runner to reclaim and merge to `main`; `053580d` is reachable in the
212: 81: shared submodule object store on this host.
213:

219: # AGENTS.md — beehive submodule overlay

221: Submodule-local rules for honeybees working `submodules/beehive/` (the beehive

225: ## Sharing modes are an invariant — do not break them
226: 
227: Beehive components converge through git in one of two modes, **detected at
228: runtime from the repo's remotes with no configuration**: *local sharing* (shared
229: checkout, keep `main` a clean projection) and *remote sharing* (private checkout,
230: push/pull; push/pull failures are fatal). A swarm may be hybrid.
231: 
232: Before touching startup, publish, remote, or checkout-health code
233: (`cmd/honeybee/main.go`, `internal/git/git.go`, `internal/swarm`), read the design
234: contract:
235: 
236: - **`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`** — the two modes, the invariants, and the
237:   startup preflight guard (reset-dirty-with-WARNING; abort-before-tokens if the
238:   checkout can't be cleaned or the pull target is unreachable).
239: 
240: Do not add a mode flag or config; mode is derived from git remotes. Never author
241: in the live shared checkout — use a worktree and converge to `main`. A honeybee
242: `preflight` WARNING/ERROR in the logs is a real defect signal, not noise.
243: 
244: </system-reminder>

# AGENTS.md — beehive submodule overlay

Submodule-local rules for honeybees working `submodules/beehive/` (the beehive
product source under `repo/`). Read alongside the repo-root `AGENTS.md` and
`HONEYBEE.md`.

## Sharing modes are an invariant — do not break them

Beehive components converge through git in one of two modes, **detected at
runtime from the repo's remotes with no configuration**: *local sharing* (shared
checkout, keep `main` a clean projection) and *remote sharing* (private checkout,
push/pull; push/pull failures are fatal). A swarm may be hybrid.

Before touching startup, publish, remote, or checkout-health code
(`cmd/honeybee/main.go`, `internal/git/git.go`, `internal/swarm`), read the design
contract:

- **`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`** — the two modes, the invariants, and the
  startup preflight guard (reset-dirty-with-WARNING; abort-before-tokens if the
  checkout can't be cleaned or the pull target is unreachable).

Do not add a mode flag or config; mode is derived from git remotes. Never author
in the live shared checkout — use a worktree and converge to `main`. A honeybee
`preflight` WARNING/ERROR in the logs is a real defect signal, not noise.

</system-reminder>

> The commit the implementer references doesn't exist in the shared object store, and the branch itself is missing entirely. The reviewer ends up searching through git internals trying to locate the work, burns through turns, times out from inactivity, and the task stays stuck in NEEDS-REVIEW. This creates a loop where every review cycle dispatches, finds nothing, stalls, and re-queues the task without resolution.
> 
> The root cause is structural: the implementer's work never actually landed because a non-fast-forward push was rejected, and per the project rules (no force-push, no origin reconciliation), the commit was left on the local branch for later reclamation. But that local branch and commit got garbage-collected, so now the review dispatcher keeps finding an unreachable task that can never be reviewed.
> 
> The fix would be to detect at review-dispatch or reclaim time that the implementer's branch is unreachable, and instead of sending a doomed review pass that idle-times-out, either bounce the task back to TODO, mark it as needing human intervention, or ensure the runner's reclaim logic catches the orphaned-push case before leaving the task in an unreviable state.

> The root cause is actually two coupled defects: first, when origin holds a divergent orphan from a prior garbage-collected attempt, the implementer's push gets rejected and the work never reaches main, yet the task still flips to NEEDS-REVIEW pointing at nothing; second, the review dispatch doesn't fast-fail on unreachable branches, so it just idles out repeatedly. 1: # session-audit-series  (seed task: session-audit-001)
2: 
3: ROI (Session self-audit → "The series" + "Output of each pass"): a recurring audit task evaluates the
4: N-2 window of completed sessions, "picks the next un-audited sessions ... never re-auditing one already
5: covered", and each pass emits an audit doc with per-session metrics + an inefficiency ranking by cost
6: saved, plus "A series of concrete fix tasks appended to PLAN.md ... The audit task only diagnoses and
7: enqueues; it does not implement the fixes itself."
8: 
9: This is the shared design doc for the whole `session-audit-NNN` series. Every pass task (`session-audit-001`,
10: `session-audit-002`, …) points here. Depends on **session-metrics-extract** for all numbers.
11: 
12: ## Why
13: The swarm should learn from its own run history and feed concrete fixes back into PLAN — continuously,
14: not once. Diagnosis is the deliverable; implementation is deferred to the enqueued fix tasks so a long
15: analytical pass never blocks on a long code change.
16: 
17: ## Priority-2 lens (ROI: "Mine sessions to simplify the protocol and cut wasted agent work")
18: This series is the mechanism for ROI Priority 2. The bar: anything an agent can be **told once, hinted
19: inline, summarized, or relieved of entirely** should be — every pass should SHRINK each agent's
20: responsibility surface. Scan each transcript through three questions and rank fixes by AGENT-TIME SAVED:
21: - Protocol confusion: agents re-deriving worktree/claim/publish rules, asking where a doc goes,
22:   mishandling submodule-vs-beehive-layer paths, or fighting git plumbing the runner is supposed to own.
23:   Each recurring confusion -> a crisper AGENTS.md rule, an inline prompt hint at the decision point, OR
24:   moving the work out of the agent into the runner.
25: - Time on unrelated tasks: turns spent on setup, exploration, or git mechanics unrelated to the actual
26:   change. Summarize/precompute it and hand the agent the result.
27: - Responsibility removal: if the runner can do something deterministically (path choices, stamping, branch
28:   naming, cleanup), take it OFF the agent's plate.
29: The enqueued fix tasks therefore skew toward prompt/AGENTS.md/runner changes (not only product code), each
30: still shipping its own design doc.
31: 
32: ## Recurrence model (no "recurring" status exists)
33: The series self-perpetuates by enqueuing its successor:
34: 1. When selected, ask `internal/audit` for the next un-audited batch in the N-2 window (two most recent
35:    sessions always excluded — they may be in-flight / under review / in arbitration and would mislead).
36: 2. If the batch is EMPTY: complete as a no-op DONE and do NOT enqueue a successor — the stream rests
37:    until new completed sessions land (a later reconcile or the next manual seed re-arms it). Do not
38:    busy-spin appending empty passes.
39: 3. If non-empty: produce the artifacts below, advance the ledger, and APPEND the successor
40:    `session-audit-<NNN+1>` [TODO] (attempts=0, deps=session-metrics-extract, Doc: this file).
41: Distinct per-pass ids are mandatory so each pass owns a unique audit doc and a unique completion/change
42: doc — never reuse an id.
43: 
44: ## Do (per pass NNN)
45: a. Run `internal/audit` over the N-2 window; get per-session metrics + per-task aggregates + the
46:    trend row for this pass. As of the six current sessions the first window (newest two excluded)
47:    covers bee-bootstrap-1782766865, links-graph-enforcement-1782767318, reconcile-1782772649,
48:    links-graph-enforcement-1782772942; the window advances as new sessions land.
49: b. Write the audit findings doc at EXACTLY `submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/session-audit-<NNN>.md`:
50:    - a per-session metrics table (turns, bytes, kind, taskid, flags);
51:    - findings with session references: why the long sessions are long; reimplementation churn
52:      (links-graph-enforcement ran three times — the canonical waste); lost-race / abandoned sessions
53:      and redundant reconcile loops; completion misses (wrong change-doc path, missing source-branch
54:      push, status left mid-phase); token/context bloat (oversized preambles, re-reading files,
55:      runner-owned git plumbing);
56:    - an inefficiency ranking ordered by estimated cost saved (turns/bytes/retries avoided);
57:    - the tracked metric trend vs prior passes (turns/bytes/retries per delivered task).
58: c. APPEND the concrete fix tasks to PLAN.md, ordered by cost saved, each `## <id> [TODO]
59:    <!-- attempts=0 deps=… weight=… -->` with a body and its OWN design doc under
60:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/tasks/<id>.md`. Tag deps where a fix rides existing code. Diagnose-and-enqueue
61:    ONLY — never implement a fix in this task.
62: d. Advance the audited ledger (mark the covered sessions audited) via `internal/audit` so it stays
63:    deterministic; leave PLAN.md parseable (plan.Parse round-trips).
64: e. Append the successor pass per the recurrence model.
65: f. Write the required completion/change doc at
66:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/bee-session-audit-<NNN>-session-audit-<NNN>.md` (the runner's completion-check
67:    path) carrying the `Beehive: session-audit-<NNN> <doc-path>` stamp; flip session-audit-<NNN> to
68:    NEEDS-REVIEW.
69: 
70: ## Verification
71: Largely analytical, but enforce: ledger advanced (covered sessions now marked audited, none
72: re-audited); every appended fix task has a matching docs/tasks/<id>.md; PLAN.md still parses; the audit
73: doc and the required completion doc both exist at their exact paths. Any helper code this pass adds
74: rides session-metrics-extract's tests.
75: 
76: ## Caveats
77: - Two-most-recent exclusion is non-negotiable; never audit a session that may still be in flight.
78: - The fix tasks are the product; resist implementing them here (a single pass that both diagnoses and
79:   fixes is exactly the long-session waste this workstream exists to kill).
80: - Cost-saved ranking is an estimate — show the inputs (turns/bytes/reruns) so it is auditable, not a
81:   bare number.
82: - Each fix task must ship its own design doc (protocol); a fix enqueued without a doc is incomplete.

(End of file - total 82 lines)
</content>

The task stays NEEDS-REVIEW and gets re-dispatched forever.
> 
> The fix puts responsibility on the runner: when a work pass's push is rejected due to a divergent orphan on origin, the runner must deterministically reconcile it by fetching that orphan and merging it with a strategy that supersedes the dead attempt, then push the new work — or if it can't land the work, it must not flip the task to NEEDS-REVIEW pointing at an unreachable commit; instead it keeps the task in a workable state.

1: # gc-source-branch-reclaim
2: 
3: ROI (Deferred features → still pending): "Pushed source-branch reclamation: honeybees now push the
4: per-task `bee-<taskid>` source branch to the submodule remote so the bumped pointer resolves for
5: peers; these branches accumulate. Reclaim them (with stale worktrees) in the GC path on DONE/cap."
6: 
7: ## Why
8: A honeybee pushes its `bee-<taskid>` branch to the submodule origin so the bumped submodule pointer
9: resolves for every other host/bee (an unpushed commit dangles the pointer). Nothing ever deletes
10: those remote branches or their local worktrees, so both accumulate without bound on the submodule
11: remote and on disk.
12: 
13: ## Do
14: Extend the GC path (rides `gc-worktree-reclaim`) on both DONE and cap:
15: - On DONE (branch merged / pointer landed on main): delete the remote `bee-<taskid>` branch on the
16:   submodule origin and reclaim any local worktree/ref.
17: - On GC of a capped/stale task: reclaim the worktree (per gc-worktree-reclaim); delete the remote
18:   branch ONLY if its pointer/merge already landed on main.
19: Add a remote-branch delete verb to internal/git (git-remote-ops `Push` with a delete refspec, e.g.
20: `git push origin --delete bee-<taskid>`), called from the swarm GC/DONE path.
21: 
22: ## Tests (swarm_test.go / git_test.go)
23: - Temp origin with a pushed `bee-x` branch + landed pointer; run DONE reclaim; assert the branch is
24:   gone on origin and the worktree reclaimed.
25: - Unmerged in-flight `bee-y`: assert it is NOT deleted.
26: - Missing/already-deleted branch: no-op, no error surfaced as failure.
27: 
28: ## Caveats
29: - Guard on "pointer/merge already on main" before any delete; deleting an unmerged branch loses work.
30: - A remote branch is safe to delete only after the submodule pointer that references its commit is
31:   itself on main — otherwise peers can no longer resolve the pointer.
32: - Shares swarm.go `Run` with worktree-sync-base / gc-worktree-reclaim; keep hunks separate, rebase
33:   on tip.

(End of file - total 33 lines)
</content>

19: active, irreversible data loss (session-transcript-finalize regressed right after pass-1's window; 97

## What this pass did
Pass 2 of the session self-audit series over the now-finalized corpus (904 files / 805 minable; 774-session N-2 window). The key move was **avoiding the series' cardinal sin** — the two largest raw sinks turned out to be already fixed, so I validated them instead of re-filing them, and enqueued only the one sink still live today.

**Partitioning the window against the landed question-tool disable (`78bd99e`, epoch 1783306971) was decisive:** every per-turn-timeout stall is pre-fix; every idle-timeout stall is post-fix — the abort mode *moved*.

- **F-FIXED-1** — interactive-elicitation stalls (171 sessions, ~15.8k turns, ~85 MB): closed by `78bd99e` (`question:false`), 0 post-fix. Not enqueued.
- **F-FIXED-2** — redundant re-review churn (editor-safety-guards 61 all-approved, etc.): closed by landed `publish-advance-guard` + `reconcile-dedup-guard` (churn stopped ~epoch 1783114). Not enqueued.
- **F-LIVE** — enqueued `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` (weight 64): a `NEEDS-REVIEW` task whose implementer commit was stranded by a non-fast-forward divergent-orphan push becomes unreachable, so every review pass spelunks and idle-times-out forever. 6 of 8 idle-timeouts are this loop; `journal-activity-stream` is **still stuck right now** (commit `053580d` absent, origin=orphan `fb3e3cfa`, 4 idle-timed-out reviews).
